summary: Strong post-update build. PDF consumption is clean — 8-step revised diagram, 4 customisation levers, paired Customer/Business Wins all land correctly. Capex was correctly held off this page. Cross-page consistency is the main drag: home-page card still carries '₹8.27 Cr capex' and names PDF authors, both of which contradict the user's no-capex / no-author directives. Within the page, partial naming drift between 'Mobile Tailor' and 'Mobile Neo Tailor' needs a clean sweep.

findings.section: index.html (home) registry data + cards + intro paragraph
findings.issue: Home page card carries '₹8.27 Cr capex · 7-day MTM · alpha pending' as the metric and the description ends 'awaiting capex + alpha sign-off' — same phrase repeated in the home intro paragraph. Contradicts the user's directive (this session) that capex stays off the register entirely.
findings.fix: Strip 'capex' from index.html:596, :627, and :782. Replace the registry metric with an operational alternative: '7-day MTM · 200 pieces / day · alpha pending'. Spec D4 in docs/dark-factory-spec.md is now stale and must also be updated.

findings.issue: Home registry desc names PDF authors: '...Concept note 30 - Apr - 2026 (Farooq Adam · Vipin Tyagi)...'. Per CLAUDE.md NEVER-do list — no Author / inline attribution lines. The dark-factory page itself correctly omits authors; the home page violates the rule.
findings.fix: Drop the parenthetical author block from index.html:782. Date can stay if it's the only thing in the parenthesis, but better to drop both and let the page-itself-as-artefact rule hold.

findings.issue: Naming drift between 'Mobile Tailor' (5 instances, residue from pre-PDF version) and 'Mobile Neo Tailor' (5 instances, new content). The PDF formalises the brand as 'Mobile Neo Tailor'; the page should land on one canonical name. M-Tailor short form is fine inside the app context.
findings.fix: Replace 'Mobile Tailor' → 'Mobile Neo Tailor' at lines 249, 269, 281, 285, 946. Keep 'M-Tailor' short form at lines 197, 332, 350, 375, 417, 425, 496 (in-app abbreviation).

findings.issue: Currency uses 'Rs. 499' / 'Rs. 243' (PDF style). Site convention per tone-of-voice §4 is the '₹' prefix (e.g., '₹3.30L Cr'). The PDF used 'Rs.' but the site convention is the rupee glyph.

The footer card 'How the levers price' quotes the explicit selling prices ('₹499 for shirt, ₹243 for T-shirt'). Per spec D6 ('No §Unit Economics on page') and per the user's broader 'showcase, not proposal' directive saved this session, explicit price-points are proposal-document content. Borderline because Trends shirt price is publicly known, but quoting it on this page tilts toward unit-economics framing.
findings.fix: Drop the rupee figures and keep the framing only: 'Standard fit at the standard Trends price · each lever shows a delta in the basket as it is opted in. Final price and the guaranteed delivery date confirmed before UPI checkout.'

findings.issue: Two new sections (§05 customisation, §10 outcomes-recast) have no status pill at the section header. Every other operational section on the page carries a 'Proposal' pill since nothing is built yet (see §02, §05 cards, §06 layout, §08 loops, §11 alpha card). New sections describe future-state behaviour and should match.
findings.fix: Add 'Proposal' pill (pill-build) at §05 H2 header and at §10 H2 header, matching the convention used in §06 line 638.

findings.issue: New YouTube demo card uses padding 'p-7' (28px). Design.md §1 paddings must be on the 4/8 rhythm: p-4 / p-5 / p-6 / p-8. (Pre-existing on lines 490, 808, 819 too — three other p-7 instances point to a system-wide drift, but this audit flags only the new one.)
findings.fix: Change 'p-7' → 'p-6' on line 422. Optionally sweep the other p-7 instances at lines 490, 808, 819 in a separate fix-pass.

findings.issue: Spec is stale. (a) §3 lists 10 sections — page now has 11. (b) D4 says headline ₹8.27 Cr stays in hero / §02 — superseded by user's new no-capex-anywhere directive. (c) §10 Out-of-scope lists 'Personalization options... defer until Apex feedback' — but §05 Customisation Levers is now on the page. (d) No mention of the YouTube embed or the Mobile Neo Tailor naming. (e) Audit history §11 has no v4 entry.
findings.fix: Update spec §3 to reflect 11 sections (insert §05 customisation and renumber §06-§11). Revise D4 to say 'no capex anywhere on the page'. Move 'Personalization options' from §10 out-of-scope to §3 active sections. Add a D14 for the YouTube embed. Add a v4 audit-history entry pointing to this audit JSON.

findings.issue: Hero now has both a deck line (14 words: 'A tailor inside the AJIO app. Designed in 2 minutes. Delivered in 7 days.') and a longer body paragraph (49 words: 'The customer-facing surface is Mobile Neo Tailor — ...'). Spec D12 says 'Hero subhead at 29 words'. The deck line passes; the body paragraph is intro prose. Document the two-line treatment in the spec so future reviewers know which is the subhead.
findings.fix: Add a D15 to docs/dark-factory-spec.md noting: 'Hero deck line is the canonical subhead (≤30 words); body paragraph below H1 is unconstrained intro prose used to introduce the Mobile Neo Tailor / Dark Factory naming pair.'

findings.issue: Card H3 is 'Watch the journey end-to-end.' — a verb-command form that addresses the reader directly. Tone-of-voice §2 says 'You is rarely used. The reader is not addressed directly.' Borderline because video labels typically use this form, but a more declarative variant fits the register better.
findings.fix: Rephrase to 'Customer journey · end-to-end demo.' or 'The 8-step flow on a phone.'

findings.issue: The line 'If the embed does not load, watch on YouTube' is mildly defensive. The iframe loads successfully on local; the disclaimer adds noise without resolving a real problem.
findings.fix: Drop the fallback line (or shorten to 'Open on YouTube ↗' as a discreet link with no apologetic preamble).

findings.issue: Mega-menu suffix is 'made-to-measure' across all 22 sibling pages. PDF formalises the brand as 'Mobile Neo Tailor'. Suffix could be tightened to 'Mobile Neo Tailor' to match the new naming.
findings.fix: If the naming change to Mobile Neo Tailor is canonical, edit tools/site_chrome.py RECENT_INNOVATIONS / AI_NATIVE entry for /dark-factory and run python tools/inject_chrome.py to sweep all sibling pages. Direct page edits will be silently reverted (per CLAUDE.md canonical-source rule).

strengths: Hero deck line lands the PDF tagline cleanly: 'A tailor inside the AJIO app. Designed in 2 minutes. Delivered in 7 days.' — 14 words, three short declaratives, exactly the register voice.

§04 8-step diagram now matches the PDF revision precisely: Discover / Companion / M-Tailor tab / Pick garment / Scan body / Adjust fit / Customise + pay / Track + receive — with PDF's metric tags carried through ('30 sec · 14 measures · ±1 cm', '3D self · 6 sliders · 3 presets').
§05 customisation levers consume all four PDF dimensions (Design / Fabric / Trims / Embroidery) with concrete material choices in each card footer — adds depth the prior page lacked without bloat.
§10 outcomes recast as paired Customer Wins / Business Wins is materially more Apex-readable than the prior 10-row table — same data, faster scan, clearer narrative.

Capex was correctly held off the page entirely per the user's new directive — even when the source PDF leads with 'Rs. 8.27 Cr' as a headline number, this page surfaces only the operational facts (200 pieces/day, 15,326 sq ft, alpha/beta/gamma capacity ramp).
